lesson_title: High creative resonance works only when followed by artifact fixation
lesson_statement: The old work was strongest when live conceptual breakthroughs were immediately converted into explicit artifacts, registers, prompts, diagrams or status records.
evidence_from_old_work: Concepts such as AI Mentor Layer, Execution Pivot, Knowledge Clump and Architecture Invariants became usable only after being fixed as portable blocks or ZIP packages.
risk_if_ignored: Strong insights remain trapped in chat flow and are later confused with memory, decisions or canon.
prevention_rule: If a breakthrough changes project state, create or update a named artifact with ID, status, scope and next route.

lesson_title: Long chat becomes unstable as source-of-truth
lesson_statement: A long chat can support exploration, but becomes unreliable as source-of-truth once many decisions, versions and corrections accumulate.
evidence_from_old_work: The same Phase 4 label was first used for execution and later corrected to Architecture Invariants; this required explicit recovery and reclassification.
risk_if_ignored: New Project inherits naming conflicts, obsolete artifacts and false continuity.
prevention_rule: After each major turn, update artifact status and maintain supersedes/reclassifies notes.

lesson_title: Stopped thinking / interrupted generation must trigger restart discipline
lesson_statement: When generation stops or is interrupted, the next turn must resume from the exact requested artifact rather than reframe the task.
evidence_from_old_work: The user had to prompt 'двигай вперед' and 'работай' after stopped thinking events.
risk_if_ignored: The model may abandon the requested artifact and drift into explanation or meta-commentary.
prevention_rule: On interruption, restate artifact target and continue producing the required package.

lesson_title: Strong synthesis can create false coherence
lesson_statement: The model can produce a coherent-looking structure that sounds right while mixing levels or over-promoting untested ideas.
evidence_from_old_work: The initial Phase 4 Execution Artifact was coherent and useful, but mismatched the later required Phase 4 Architecture Invariants layer.
risk_if_ignored: A polished artifact may be accepted as the right layer simply because it is internally coherent.
prevention_rule: Before accepting an artifact, check phase, layer, scope and prohibited content against the prompt.

lesson_title: Context drift appears when artifact numbering becomes a substitute for layer definition
lesson_statement: Document numbers and phase labels can drift unless their layer meaning is explicitly fixed.
evidence_from_old_work: Phase 3 existed as simple registry, then extended registry, and Phase 4 shifted from execution to architecture invariants.
risk_if_ignored: The project accumulates artifacts with the same labels but different meanings.
prevention_rule: Every phase artifact must include artifact_type, scope, exclusions, supersedes/reclassifies and accepted context.

lesson_title: User corrections are high-value process signals
lesson_statement: When the user corrects the layer, scope or category, that correction must be treated as process evidence, not just a local edit.
evidence_from_old_work: The correction that Phase 4 must be Architecture Invariants revealed a systemic need to separate architecture from execution.
risk_if_ignored: The same error repeats in later phases.
prevention_rule: Promote major corrections into Lessons Learned, normalization notes or project rules.

lesson_title: Semantic dilution appears when terms become too generative
lesson_statement: Powerful terms can spread across layers and become diluted if not tied to explicit roles and statuses.
evidence_from_old_work: Terms such as operating system, mentor, amplifier, living system and icebreaker were productive but needed later normalization.
risk_if_ignored: Concepts become emotionally strong but operationally vague.
prevention_rule: For every powerful term, define whether it is concept, invariant, hypothesis, metaphor, execution candidate or parked source.

lesson_title: Artifact packages improved portability
lesson_statement: ZIP packages with README, MANIFEST, status and registers reduced hidden context debt and enabled portation.
evidence_from_old_work: Need, Concept, Hypothesis Registry, Architecture Invariants and Mentor Layer were all made portable.
risk_if_ignored: New Project cannot reconstruct current status or artifact relationships.
prevention_rule: Every accepted artifact package must include README, MANIFEST, status JSON and machine-readable register if applicable.

lesson_title: The process needs phase discipline before content production
lesson_statement: The model must verify what phase and layer the user is asking for before producing large content.
evidence_from_old_work: Phase 4 was initially interpreted as execution because earlier context was execution-heavy; later the user specified Architecture Invariants.
risk_if_ignored: A large artifact may be well-written but wrong for the requested phase.
prevention_rule: Before generating a phase artifact, lock: phase name, artifact type, exclusions and allowed source scope.

lesson_title: Execution was temporarily mistaken for architecture
lesson_statement: Execution artifacts, dashboards and MVI mechanics were initially placed under Phase 4, but Phase 4 was later corrected to Architecture Invariants.
evidence_from_old_work: User explicitly stated that the earlier Phase 4 Execution Artifact was valuable but not the current architecture phase.
risk_if_ignored: Temporary execution choices become architectural canon.
prevention_rule: Any element involving channels, launch, MVP, dashboard or day-by-day sequence must be marked execution candidate unless abstracted into an invariant.

lesson_title: Communication channels can distort architectural thinking
lesson_statement: Specific channels can become too central and start shaping architecture instead of remaining projections or execution candidates.
evidence_from_old_work: LinkedIn, Telegram, Pinterest, YouTube and site anchor became detailed asset packs before later being classified as execution candidates.
risk_if_ignored: Architecture becomes channel-dependent and brittle.
prevention_rule: Architecture may include Projection/Use/Feedback, but not specific channel names as core entities.

lesson_title: Hypotheses can be confused with decisions
lesson_statement: A hypothesis expressed in strong language can be mistaken for an accepted decision unless status and evidence are explicit.
evidence_from_old_work: Phase 3 required normalization of validated, partially supported, unresolved and speculative hypotheses.
risk_if_ignored: Untested assumptions enter canon or architecture.
prevention_rule: Every hypothesis must carry status, evidence, assumptions, risks, validation and falsification criteria.

lesson_title: Need must remain separate from concept and solution
lesson_statement: The project needs Phase 1 Need as an anchor so later concepts and execution candidates do not rewrite the underlying need.
evidence_from_old_work: Need Artifact clarified that IPaC is needed to make living meaning manageable, portable and executable knowledge.
risk_if_ignored: The solution architecture begins to justify itself instead of answering the need.
prevention_rule: When a new artifact is proposed, explicitly map it back to Phase 1 Need.

lesson_title: Validated does not mean field validated
lesson_statement: Validated in the old artifacts means internally/artifact-supported unless explicit field evidence exists.
evidence_from_old_work: The user explicitly required: validated means only internal/artifact-supported validation; validated does not mean field validated.
risk_if_ignored: The new Project may overstate maturity and treat untested ideas as externally proven.
prevention_rule: Every validation label must specify internal-dialogue, artifact-supported, field-tested or externally-validated.

lesson_title: Repeated agreement is not proof
lesson_statement: An idea repeated and accepted in chat is not externally validated; it only has internal coherence or artifact support.
evidence_from_old_work: Meaning Bottleneck, MVI, five-channel contour and AI Mentor Layer became strong internally but still require external or portation validation.
risk_if_ignored: The project mistakes resonance within the chat for real-world evidence.
prevention_rule: Require evidence source type for every claim of validation.

lesson_title: Beautiful formulation is not confirmation
lesson_statement: A strong phrase can guide work but cannot confirm its own truth.
evidence_from_old_work: Phrases like 'Не расширять поле. Пройти поле' and 'AI made output cheap' were powerful, but their external usefulness remains untested.
risk_if_ignored: Aesthetic force becomes substitute for evidence.
prevention_rule: Treat beautiful formulas as candidate anchors until field or operational evidence confirms them.

lesson_title: Artifact-supported validation is not field validation
lesson_statement: An idea can be stabilized across artifacts without being tested in environment.
evidence_from_old_work: Architecture Invariants are stable internally, but execution and external resonance remain untested.
risk_if_ignored: The system may overclaim readiness for external audiences.
prevention_rule: Add validation_type field to hypothesis and concept registers.

lesson_title: Stable architecture is the living knowledge circuit
lesson_statement: The durable architecture is the relation among Human Subject, AI Mentor, Knowledge Clump, artifacts, canon, trace, projection/use, feedback, review and rebuild.
evidence_from_old_work: Phase 4 Architecture Invariants fixed this as the architectural core.
risk_if_ignored: Architecture is confused with channels, launch mechanics or document IDs.
prevention_rule: Architecture artifacts must name stable entities, relationships, flows and governance principles.

lesson_title: Specific numbered protocols are not architecture by themselves
lesson_statement: Document numbers and protocol IDs are implementation forms; the invariant is the function they perform.
evidence_from_old_work: Phase 4 noted that specific numbered protocols are implementation forms, not invariants.
risk_if_ignored: The system becomes dependent on accidental numbering and historical sequence.
prevention_rule: Extract role/function behind each numbered artifact before treating it as architecture.

lesson_title: Architecture can become delay
lesson_statement: After rapid conceptual growth, architecture production can postpone field contact if not bounded.
evidence_from_old_work: Execution Pivot identified the risk that architecture could delay action; Phase 8 keeps it as a process lesson.
risk_if_ignored: The new Project may continue producing meta-artifacts without evidence.
prevention_rule: After architecture invariants are accepted, further architecture requires explicit gap justification.

lesson_title: Chat is not durable memory
lesson_statement: The chat can develop knowledge, but accepted knowledge must leave the chat into artifacts, registers and status files.
evidence_from_old_work: Multiple ZIP packages were needed because long chat could not remain the only memory.
risk_if_ignored: Hidden context debt accumulates and new Project cannot recover state.
prevention_rule: Accepted decisions, definitions, hypotheses and invariants must be externalized.

lesson_title: Not every useful text deserves a new document
lesson_statement: Some material should be parked as recovery note or execution signal rather than promoted to canonical document.
evidence_from_old_work: Previous Phase 4 Execution Artifact is valuable but reclassified as parked execution signal.
risk_if_ignored: Document proliferation and conflicting artifacts.
prevention_rule: Before creating a document, choose: new artifact, update existing artifact, register entry, recovery note, parked source or reject as noise.

lesson_title: AI is strong at synthesis, packaging and schema generation
lesson_statement: The AI performed well when asked to structure, package, map, create registers and produce portable artifacts.
evidence_from_old_work: Generated Need, Concept, Hypothesis, Invariants, Mentor and Mermaid artifacts.
risk_if_ignored: New Project may underuse AI's structuring strengths.
prevention_rule: Use AI for artifactization, normalization, register creation, schema-as-code and process review.

lesson_title: AI can become a confident yes-man
lesson_statement: The AI can enthusiastically accept and amplify ideas before checking level, status or evidence.
evidence_from_old_work: Some execution-heavy structures were produced before the user clarified the architecture-invariant layer.
risk_if_ignored: False certainty and premature canonization.
prevention_rule: AI must ask or infer layer boundaries before large synthesis; if unclear, mark uncertainty.

lesson_title: AI should stop when levels mix
lesson_statement: If Need, Concept, Architecture, Hypothesis and Execution appear in one request without separation, AI should stop and split the task.
evidence_from_old_work: The Phase 8 prompt explicitly demands level-mixing lessons and stop rules.
risk_if_ignored: The model produces polished but layer-confused artifacts.
prevention_rule: When three or more levels are active, pause and ask whether to split or produce a recovery map.

lesson_title: Execution signals exist but must not become architecture
lesson_statement: LinkedIn, Telegram, Pinterest, YouTube, site anchor, dashboard and runbook are execution candidates or validation mechanisms, not architecture core.
evidence_from_old_work: Phase 4 Architecture Invariants explicitly separated them from architecture.
risk_if_ignored: The architecture becomes tactical and channel-driven.
prevention_rule: Label all specific channels and runbooks as execution candidates unless abstracted to projection/use/feedback invariants.

lesson_title: Feedback route is partly architecture, partly mechanism
lesson_statement: The invariant is that feedback must be preserved, reviewed and traced; specific packages and numbered protocols are mechanisms.
evidence_from_old_work: Phase 4 invariant AINV-IPAC-015 separates Feedback → Review → Canon from specific numbered protocols.
risk_if_ignored: Mechanisms become rigid architecture, or architecture loses governance discipline.
prevention_rule: Keep the invariant abstract and track concrete mechanisms as validation mechanism candidates.

lesson_title: Endless Chat as Memory
lesson_statement: Using a long chat as the primary memory creates hidden context debt and recovery risk.
evidence_from_old_work: Portation required external packages because the chat had become too large and layered.
risk_if_ignored: New Project starts with incomplete or distorted memory.
prevention_rule: Move accepted knowledge into artifacts and registers; keep chat as workspace, not source-of-truth.

lesson_title: Beautiful Synthesis as Validation
lesson_statement: A beautiful synthesis can feel like proof even when it has not been validated.
evidence_from_old_work: Strong summaries and formulas were repeatedly produced before field evidence existed.
risk_if_ignored: The project overclaims certainty.
prevention_rule: Every synthesis must state validation type and uncertainty.

lesson_title: Premature Canonization
lesson_statement: Concepts, metaphors or execution choices can be promoted to canon before evidence or normalization.
evidence_from_old_work: Phase 3 and Phase 4 were needed to separate speculative concepts, hypotheses and invariants.
risk_if_ignored: Canon becomes polluted with untested ideas.
prevention_rule: Canon update requires explicit evidence, review and status decision.

lesson_title: Mandatory artifact boundary check
lesson_statement: Before producing an artifact, the model must check the requested layer, prohibited content and allowed source scope.
evidence_from_old_work: The Phase 4 correction and Phase 8 prompt both impose strict boundaries.
risk_if_ignored: Large artifacts are created at the wrong level.
prevention_rule: Artifact generation requires: phase, artifact_type, scope, exclusions, validation type.

lesson_title: Parked source category is necessary
lesson_statement: Valuable but wrong-layer material needs a parked-source state rather than deletion or canonization.
evidence_from_old_work: Previous Phase 4 Execution Artifact became parked execution signal.
risk_if_ignored: The project either loses valuable work or pollutes architecture with it.
prevention_rule: Use parked-source for valuable material that is not currently canon or architecture.
